zotero.abstractNote: Purpose: To design and construct a 32-channel multi-coil setup for dynamic B0 shimming of the human brain at 9.4T. Methods: The multi-coil array consists of 32 circular coils arranged on a cylindrical former. The coil geometry was optimized using a genetic algorithm to maximize the shimming performance over the human brain volume. The coils were driven by a custom-built multi-channel current source. The performance of the multi-coil setup was evaluated in phantom and in vivo experiments at 9.4T. Results: The multi-coil setup provides a significant improvement in B0 homogeneity compared to the standard spherical harmonic shimming. The in vivo results demonstrate that the multi-coil setup can effectively compensate for B0 field inhomogeneities induced by the human brain. Conclusion: The 32-channel multi-coil setup is a promising tool for high-field MRI, enabling improved B0 shimming and potentially enhancing the quality of high-field MRI applications.
